_note: Estimated benchmarks — run `go test -bench=. ./pkg/jwt/` for real numbers on your hardware

results.note: HMAC-SHA256 is a single pass over the signing input. Pure CPU-bound cryptography, no I/O. The bottleneck is SHA-256 compression rounds.
Verify re-runs HMAC (same cost as sign) plus constant-time comparison. Marginally slower than sign due to the constant-time comparison overhead.
RSA sign = modular exponentiation with the private exponent (d, ~2048 bits). ~140× slower than HS256. This is why auth servers issue long-lived tokens — signing is expensive.
RSA verify uses the public exponent e=65537 (only 17 bits set). Much cheaper than sign. ~13× faster than sign, which is why CDN/API gateways can verify RS256 tokens at high volume.

insight: The RS256 sign/verify asymmetry (8,500 vs 110,000 ops/sec) is why OAuth2 auth servers cache signed tokens instead of signing on every request. The issuer signs once; verifiers check millions of times.
